description: A baked layered pasta dish made with sheets of pasta, sauce, cheese, and commonly meat or vegetables. It is assembled in layers and baked until hot, set, and lightly browned on top.
preparation_steps: Prepare the sauce and any meat or vegetable filling.
Layer pasta sheets with sauce, cheese, and fillings in a baking dish.
Repeat layers until the dish is assembled.
Bake until bubbling and cooked through.
Rest briefly before slicing and serving.

health_advice.benefits: Can be filling and substantial, and vegetable-based versions can add more fiber and micronutrients.
health_advice.risks: Large portions can be high in calories, sodium, and saturated fat because of cheese and meat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate cheese, add vegetables, and keep portions sensible.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional lasagna usually contains wheat pasta and cheese. Vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free versions are common with substitutions.
